source_identity.case_name: Cultural Landscape Foundation v. U.S. Department of the Interior
source_identity.declarant_title: Deputy Director for Operations, National Park Service

extracted_facts_compressed: The Lincoln Memorial Reflecting Pool was drained and a tinted polyurea liner was installed for waterproofing and protection before refilling ahead of U.S. 250th-related events.
On 2026-06-09, NPS reported damage and U.S. Park Police responded.
The declaration describes caulk over foam sealant cut with a sharp knife or razor.
The declaration describes destruction of delaminating surface material.
The declaration states approximately 70 fence post tops were thrown into the pool.
NPS planned to drain the pool again after July 4 for repairs and assessment of the lining.
Frank Lands signed under penalty of perjury, digitally executed 2026-06-24 at 18:09.

supported_by_declaration: Official report of localized sharp-implement damage to caulk/foam sealant.
Official observation of delaminating surface material destruction.
Official observation of fence post tops thrown into the pool.
not_resolved_by_declaration: Whether a 300-350 foot gash claim is supported by primary evidence.
Whether algae was caused by vandalism, water chemistry, nanobubbler removal, sunlight, construction disturbance, or maintenance failure.
Whether coating/liner delamination was caused by vandalism, rushed rehabilitation, substrate issues, water intrusion, or normal material failure.
Whether photos, surveillance, contractor reports, or arrest details confirm the full damage timeline.

receiptos_mapping.signal_core: The Frank Lands declaration is a primary public-record source for the official NPS account of reflecting pool damage after rehabilitation.
receiptos_mapping.acceleration_path: Use as a Public Records Receipt template: docket metadata, source URL, declarant verification, exact excerpt, timeline, narrative delta, and hash gate status.
receiptos_mapping.narrative_delta: The declaration supports localized sharp-implement damage and related vandalism, but does not itself prove broader public claims about large linear gashes, algae causation, or renovation quality failures.

claim_effect.reason: Extraction improves structured readability and narrative-delta handling but does not provide PDF byte hash or photo evidence.
